<!--
  Before opening: read CONTRIBUTING.md.
  A PR is only reviewed if it closes an issue a maintainer labeled `approved`,
  or if a maintainer opened it. PRs without an approved issue will be closed
  without review — this just means the change wasn't agreed to first.
-->

## Linked approved issue

Closes #<!-- issue number that carries the `approved` label -->

## What this changes

<!-- One or two sentences. Keep the PR focused on the single approved issue. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] This PR references an issue **labeled `approved`** above (or I am a maintainer).
- [ ] Commits are **signed** and **signed off** (`git commit -s -S`).
- [ ] I ran the tool's tests (`npm run test`) and they pass locally.
- [ ] Any changed figure cites its official source in the sourced config (no unsourced numbers).
- [ ] No advocacy language; the change stays neutral and sourced.
